description: Twist tags introduce a constraint, paradox, or unexpected property that pushes items out of their default shape. One is assigned per item slot. They are directional — the LLM interprets how the twist applies to the specific item type and guild context.
twist_tags: leaves evidence of use
works only when the bearer is observed
remembers its last owner
gets heavier the longer it is carried
works backwards — the cost comes before the effect
stronger when the bearer is afraid
only functions when broken or damaged
the effect is invisible to the bearer but visible to others
requires an audience to function
bonds to the first person who touches it
knows when it is being lied about
the effect fades if the bearer speaks about it
works better the further from home the bearer is
has already been used once — and remembers
the item chooses when to activate, not the bearer
effect is stronger in darkness, weaker in light
leaves a mark on the bearer that does not fade
can only be used once per moon cycle
the bearer cannot voluntarily discard it
works only on the third attempt
amplifies the bearer's worst quality alongside the intended effect
the effect is delayed — activates at an unknown later moment
slowly changes the bearer's appearance over time
only functions in silence
the item is aware of the bearer's intentions
effect scales with how much the bearer has lost recently
must be given away to retain its power — cannot be kept
the bearer forgets owning it between uses
gets weaker each time it succeeds
works perfectly — but only for someone else, never the owner
the effect is contagious — spreads to those nearby
requires the bearer to have done something irreversible recently
leaves a sound behind in the place it was used
the item has a name and responds to being called by it
effect is stronger the more people witness it being used
can be sensed by animals before humans notice it
the bearer dreams of the item's previous uses while sleeping
only activates when the bearer genuinely does not need it
the effect is permanent the first time, temporary every time after
works in opposition to the bearer's stated intention
becomes inert when two of its kind are within range of each other
the effect is transferred to whoever next touches the bearer
functions differently depending on which hand holds it
the item is older than it should be possible for it to be
leaves no trace of ever having existed after it is destroyed
the bearer cannot describe what it does, only demonstrate it
effect is doubled when the bearer is outnumbered
the item repairs itself, but never quite back to its original form
works only for someone who does not know its true purpose
the cost is paid by whoever witnesses the effect, not the bearer
